$comment: Normatively defined by the AIP Agent Identity Object rules. Canonical signing field order for previous_key_signature: aid, name, type, model, created_at, version, public_key, previous_key_signature (set to '' during signing).
title: AIP Core Identity Object
description: The persistent identity record for an AIP agent. Signing order for previous_key_signature: (aid, name, type, model, created_at, version, public_key, previous_key_signature) with previous_key_signature set to empty string during signing.

aid.description: W3C DID-conformant Agent Identity. MUST match the did:aip ABNF. Format: did:aip:<namespace>:<32-hex-chars>, where the 32-hex agent-id is the leftmost 128 bits of SHA-256 over the decoded Ed25519 public key. Both namespace and agent-id MUST be lowercase only.

name.description: Human-readable agent name. MUST NOT be used as a unique identifier — the aid field is the only stable identity.

type.description: Agent type. MUST exactly match the namespace component of the agent's aid. The namespace MUST be an active, non-reserved entry in the synced AIP Namespace Catalog unless the Registry explicitly documents a private local namespace policy.

model.description: The AI model backing this agent. Recorded for transparency and auditability.

provider.description: Name or identifier of the model provider (e.g., 'anthropic', 'openai', 'mistral'). MUST NOT be empty.

model_id.description: Model identifier as defined by the provider (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'gpt-4o'). MUST NOT be empty.

attestation_hash.description: OPTIONAL for Tier 1, SHOULD be present for Tier 2, and REQUIRED by Registry registration validation for Tier 3. SHA-256 hash of the model binary or version manifest, prefixed with 'sha256:'. Used for verifiable model pinning and model-substitution detection in high-assurance deployments.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of agent creation. Set at initial registration and MUST NOT be modified thereafter (immutable field).

version.description: Monotonically increasing version counter. Starts at 1 on initial registration. MUST increment by exactly 1 on each key rotation. No other field changes are permitted — the only valid reason to increment version is key rotation.

public_key.description: The agent's current public key in JWK format per RFC 7517. MUST be an Ed25519 key (kty=OKP, crv=Ed25519) per RFC 8037. This protocol version does not define ES256 or RS256 Agent Identity keys.

kty.description: Key type. MUST be OKP for Ed25519 per RFC 8037.

x.description: Base64url-encoded public key bytes (32 bytes encodes to exactly 43 base64url characters with no padding). Case-sensitive.

kid.description: Key ID — a DID URL fragment uniquely identifying this key version. Format: <aid>#key-<n> where n starts at 1 and increments by 1 on each rotation. #key-0 is not valid. Must exactly match the kid in Credential Token headers signed by this key.

previous_key_signature.description: OPTIONAL on initial registration (version=1). REQUIRED on key rotation (version>=2). Base64url-encoded EdDSA signature proving continuity of identity across key rotation. Computed by serializing the complete new Agent Identity Object using the Section 2.1 canonical JSON procedure with previous_key_signature set to empty string '', then signing with the retiring previous private key. The computed signature replaces the empty string in the stored object. Verifiers retrieve the immediately previous public key from /v1/agents/{aid}/public-key/{key-id} to verify; signing with the new private key does not satisfy this continuity proof.
REQUIRED for version >= 2. Must be a non-empty base64url string.
